top_signals.title: JPY/USD: Why Is Japan Yen So Weak? Can Government Intervention Help Currency?
top_signals.summary: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Kazakhstan Suspends Major Oil Exports as Black Sea Risks Escalate
top_signals.summary: Following a drone attack on the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) terminal along the Black Sea Coast, Kazakhstan has newly confirmed a halt to crude transfers there. "Kazakhstan is set to stop piping crude to a port on Russia’s Black Sea coast after a spate of attacks on tankers that's jeopardizing the landlocked Asian country’s ability to produce oil," Bloomberg reports Tuesday morning. "The CPC Terminal at the Russian port of Novorossiysk will stop accepting piped supplies because tanker companies are too nervous to…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Companies race to deliver autonomous uncrewed fighter jets
top_signals.summary: The U.S. Air Force has said it expects aircraft in its ‌program to cost ‌a third, or less, of the cost of an F-35 fighter jet, which can cost $80 million or more.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
